title: Airplanes

vocabulary.meaning: to leave the ground and begin flying
vocabulary.example: I still get excited when the plane starts to take off.

vocabulary.meaning: to arrive on the ground without problems
vocabulary.example: Most passengers just want the flight to land safely and on time.

vocabulary.meaning: a seat next to the window on a plane
vocabulary.example: I usually choose a window seat because I like the view.

vocabulary.meaning: a very long flight, usually between distant countries
vocabulary.example: I try to sleep during a long-haul flight.

vocabulary.meaning: tiredness after a long flight across time zones
vocabulary.example: Jet lag can make the first day of a trip difficult.

vocabulary.meaning: sudden movement of the plane caused by unstable air
vocabulary.example: I do not like air turbulence, but I know it is usually not dangerous.
